what_it_does: Defines the frozen P0.3 format for a machine-checked, embedding-ready UI contract corpus that a UI-building AI consumes instead of reading repo code or harness UI. It specifies file layout, entry and operation templates, validation rules, coverage mapping, navigation protocol, code-side build obligations, and a ledger of how prior critiques were resolved.
observations: Frozen at status P0.3 with a no-versioning law: entry content updates in place, format changes only via recorded decisions edited into the body plus a changelog line.
Adjudicates a 3-lens dialectic and declares a resource-oriented spine, while grafting rules from the event-sourced and capability-verbs lenses.
Provides a complete file layout for ~/company/ui-contract/ including authored, generated, and evidence directories.
Includes a full operation template and worked example for session.post, showing op metadata, schemas, errors, consequences, examples, and adjacent-op links.
Lists 26 validation rules (V1–V26) split into static and reality-join categories, intended to be enforced by tools/validate.py.
Defines a status/verification lifecycle: planned, building, live, broken, retired, with harness-written per-branch verification and evidence replay.
Mandates generated registries (exposure.json, reality.json, coverage.json, TASKS.md, INDEX.md) and forbids hand-editing generated files.
Requires a two-layer coverage map: contracted claims versus demonstrated proof from blind-author task verdicts.
Contains a long dialectic resolutions ledger (§10) mapping every named critique finding to a specific rule or section.
Imposes code-side obligations on the fabric build (OPS constants, route registries, uniform error envelope, event emit helper, address schemes) so machine checks have real ground truth.
standouts: The format is designed as a contract for a system still under build, with explicit planned/building/live honesty rather than pretending the system already exists.
It treats critique resolution as a first-class artifact: §10 records how each finding was addressed, making the design rationale inspectable.
Validation is deliberately two-sided: static checks plus a reality join that fails loud if the live system is unavailable.
The worked session.post example is marked planned/synthetic and explicitly lists which consequence branches are verified, unverified, or probe-verified.
conventions: Uses frontmatter metadata (type, resource, summary, schemes, status, relates-to) for every entry.
Wikilink-style internal references such as [[session#op: session.list]] and [[session-message]].
Uniform op verb vocabulary: list, get, create, update, delete, act, watch, search.
contract:op, contract:schema, contract:error, contract:example, contract:fsm fenced code blocks as machine-readable anchors.
Bold one-sentence restatement as the first line of every H2 section.
Generated files carry generated: headers and are overwritten by tools; hand-edits fail validation.
Append-only evidence ledger under evidence/runs/ and coverage/drops.jsonl.
concerns: The document is extremely dense and interdependent; a reader must hold many cross-references and validation rules in working memory.
Many rules assume tooling (validate.py, extract_reality.py, coverage.py, rename.py) that is specified but not shown in this file.
The format imposes numerous code-side obligations on an unbuilt fabric; if those obligations are not met, V22–V26 cannot run.
Status flips and verification rows are meant to be harness-written, but the file does not show the harness logic or how it avoids hand-edits.
Large sections (e.g., §10) are inlined into the body, which helps avoid drift but makes the file itself long and harder to scan.
notes: A frozen, self-modifying specification that tries to make a UI contract corpus machine-checkable, honest about build status, and navigable by an autonomous agent without reading source code.
questions: Is tools/validate.py already implemented and green, or is it part of the F1 lane obligation?
How does the harness actually perform evidence replay and write verification rows without human editing?
What is the exact relationship between this corpus-root file and the build-prep Session Fabric documents it cites?
How are the 35 FEATURE-ATLAS classes (CC-01…CC-35) defined and kept in sync with this format?
What happens when a planned binding appears in reality.json but the op is still marked planned—does the checker block, warn, or auto-flip?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define a frozen contract format for a UI-building AI, and the file does exactly that—perhaps more: it also embeds adjudication rationale, build obligations, and a critique-resolution ledger, making it part spec, part design record, and part build checklist.
apparent_themes: Machine-checkable contracts as a substitute for code-reading.
Single source of truth with generated artifacts and no hand-edited generated files.
Adversarial design review captured as a durable ledger.
Traceability from affordance to operation to reality to evidence.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a durable, self-policing contract surface between a complex fabric system and a UI-building agent, so that the agent can discover, invoke, and verify operations without ever touching the implementation, while the format itself resists the usual drift between docs, schemas, and live code.
novelty: It combines a resource-oriented contract corpus with event-sourced proof shapes and capability-verb task indexing, then encodes the design dialectic and all critique resolutions directly into the format document as a frozen, in-place-evolving artifact.
contribution: A detailed, machine-oriented contract-format schema with explicit status semantics, validation-rule vocabulary, generated-artifact discipline, and a critique-resolution ledger that a fused system could reuse to keep documentation, schemas, and reality checks aligned.
summary_for_embedding: A frozen P0.3 specification for the ~/company/ui-contract corpus format, defining resource-oriented contract entries, operation templates, validation rules, coverage mapping, navigation protocol, build obligations, and a ledger of resolved design critiques.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a machine-checked, embedding-ready contract surface that lets a UI-building AI discover and verify fabric operations without reading source code, while keeping the format honest about what is planned, building, live, or broken.
